docs: 更新 README，去掉版本历史，更新交互界面截图为 v0.2.8 无边框样式

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: detect WiX bundle entries in pre-clean; delete CPython-3.14 dependency key

Root cause of 'action: Modify' mode:
- Python 3.14.2 (64-bit) GUID {0ef3959c...} is a WiX Burn bundle ARP entry
- msiexec /x returns 1605 on bundles (not an MSI product) which was excluded
  from force-deletion, so the ARP key survived every pre-clean run
- Burn finds this ARP key on next launch -> enters Modify mode
- In Modify mode BA only requests core+exe, not lib_AllUsers -> no stdlib

Fix:
- Detect bundle entries by presence of BundleVersion registry value
- Skip msiexec for bundles; directly Remove-Item the ARP key
- After registry sweep, also delete the Burn dependency provider key
  HKLM/HKCU ..\Installer\Dependencies\CPython-3.14 which is the actual
  trigger for Burn's 'bundle is installed' detection

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(mobile): Register flow with API authentication (#55)

* Initial plan

* feat(mobile): Add register flow with API authentication

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/nelson-arbelaez-epam/todos/sessions/19af5e2b-1483-42e6-85bf-583cd2f00134

Co-authored-by: nelson-arbelaez-epam <273367797+nelson-arbelaez-epam@users.noreply.github.com>

* Initial plan

Co-authored-by: nelson-arbelaez-epam <273367797+nelson-arbelaez-epam@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(mobile): apply ADR-0024 session store pattern with Zustand and @todos/core DTOs

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/nelson-arbelaez-epam/todos/sessions/c0a58ca3-7dae-4b96-a5f4-d1dc6691e8f1

Co-authored-by: nelson-arbelaez-epam <273367797+nelson-arbelaez-epam@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(mobile): align register flow env and test setup

* fix(mobile): simplify RegisterScreen as pure form presenter

Remove the in-screen success branch and onNavigateToLogin prop so
RegisterScreen is a pure form presenter. Post-registration redirect
is now owned by the Expo Router layer, mirroring web's Register page
pattern and ADR-0024 session-state rules.

* chore: exclude Expo auto-generated files from Biome checks

.expo/ and expo-env.d.ts are generated by the Expo CLI on every
start/build. They carry a 'do not edit' notice and must not be
reformatted by Biome.

* test(mobile): configure vitest alias for expo-router routes

* test(mobile): add unit tests for register flow components and routes

- RegisterForm: validation (empty email, short password) and submit behavior
- RegisterScreen: store wiring for isLoading, error, and register action
- Home route (/): redirect to /register when no session exists
- Register route (/register): redirect to / when already authenticated

* Require mobile API base URL

Fail fast in the mobile auth client when EXPO_PUBLIC_TODOS_API_URL is missing and align the documented default API port with the current local contract.

* Clean up MCP README boilerplate

Remove stale NestJS template reference lines and normalize the install snippet without mixing that cleanup into the mobile env-contract change.

* Pin Vitest versions across workspaces

Replace floating and divergent Vitest ranges with a single explicit workspace version so installs and lockfile refreshes stay deterministic.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test(aipm): cover ignore-only rule override branch in load_lint_config

the short-circuit condition on line 835:

This branch is only reached when `level` is None but the rule table
has a non-empty `ignore` array. All existing tests either set a
`level` (short-circuiting before this sub-expression) or use only
instead). The new test uses a minimal aipm.toml with only
`ignore = ["examples/**", "docs/**"]` to hit this gap.

Branch coverage: 94.36% → 94.41% (119 → 118 missed).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add thinktradehub.com routing to Caddy

Caddy now owns ports 80/443 and routes both domains:
- paperpilotapp.com → PaperPilot frontend/backend (internal Docker network)
- thinktradehub.com → Phoenix services via host.docker.internal:
    /api/* /auth/* /health → phoenix-api:8011
    /ws/* → phoenix-ws-gateway:8031
    /* → phoenix-dashboard:3000

extra_hosts: host.docker.internal:host-gateway allows Caddy to
reach Phoenix's published host ports from inside its container.
Caddy auto-issues Let's Encrypt certs for both domains.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add critical: database credentials are incorrect on Render

The system is 100% built but can't function without valid DB credentials.
This file explains:
1. What's wrong (28P01 authentication error)
2. Why it matters (can't create accounts)
3. What to do (update 5 environment variables)
4. How to verify it works

Root cause: DB_USER and/or DB_PASSWORD don't match the actual PostgreSQL
service on Render. Need user to provide correct credentials.

commit.message:
fix: Caddy directly on 80/443, evict port squatters on deploy

Root cause: Phoenix process binds port 80 → Traefik crashes on start.
Fix: bypass Traefik entirely, Caddy owns 80/443 directly.

- Deploy script kills any Docker container or system process on 80/443
  before starting Caddy (using docker ps + fuser)
- Caddy: ports 80/443/443udp (handles TLS + HTTP→HTTPS redirect)
- Caddyfile: paperpilotapp.com domain → Caddy auto-issues Let's Encrypt cert
- No external network dependencies

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add resizable window support to l_gfx.h

Windows GDI: Remove WS_THICKFRAME/WS_MAXIMIZEBOX restriction, add
WM_SIZE handler that reallocates the DIB at the new size.

X11: Handle ConfigureNotify (event 22) to detect resize, reallocate
pixel buffer via l_mmap/l_munmap at new dimensions.

Linux framebuffer: Not resizable (hardware-fixed resolution).

New API: l_canvas_resized(c) — returns 1 if window was resized since
last call, clears the flag. width/height/stride/pixels are already
updated when this returns true. Apps using game-loop patterns adapt
naturally by reading c->width/c->height each frame.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(codec): use reflect padding in pad_to_hop_length to match Python DACVAE._pad

Python's DACVAE._pad uses torch.nn.functional.pad with mode='reflect',
not zero-padding. Burn's PadMode::Reflect uses the same convention as
PyTorch (padded[N+k] = x[N-2-k]), so switching from Constant(0.0) back
to Reflect fixes the encoder parity for the second half of frames.

E2E result: all 13 frames now pass with mean_abs_err ~4e-6 (float32
precision), max_abs_err 3.4e-5. Parity check PASSES with atol=1e-4.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: follow symlinks when reading model file sizes

DirEntry::metadata() which returns the symlink inode size.

instead of propagating the error and returning an empty catalog.
